title: Sentinel Shield release manifest
description: A canonical, deterministically-serialized fingerprint of exactly WHAT a release ships: the source commit and its git tree hash, the tag target, the release scope, the CI workflow runs, the verified artifact digests, the workflow action pins, the tool versions, and the profile-policy and schema digests. The document is split into a hashed `body` (the reproducible content) and a NON-hashed `metadata` section (timestamps and tool-environment provenance that must NOT perturb the reproducibility hash). `reproducibility.hash` is the SHA-256 of the canonical serialization of `body` alone (jq -S -c, i.e. recursively key-sorted, compact); regenerating from the same repository state yields an identical hash, and any tamper to `body` is detected because the stored hash no longer matches. Contains NO secrets.

metadata.description: NON-hashed provenance. Excluded from reproducibility.hash by construction so timestamps and tool-environment differences never change the reproducible fingerprint.

generated_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC time the manifest was generated. NOT hashed.

body.description: The reproducible content. reproducibility.hash covers exactly this object, canonicalized with jq -S -c.

tree_hash.description: git tree hash of source_commit (rev-parse <commit>^{tree}); 'unknown' when not locally resolvable.

tag_target.description: The commit the release tag points at (release_commit, or source_commit when they are the same).

workflow_runs.description: The engine CI runs that back the release, sorted by run_id.

artifact_digests.description: SHA-256 digests of the verified release artifacts, sorted by (run_id, artifact_id).

action_pins.description: Every workflow `uses:` reference (engine CI + consumer templates), de-duplicated and sorted, so a moved/mutated action pin changes the fingerprint.

source.description: workflow file the reference was found in

tool_versions.description: Versions of the tools that produced the release. Part of the provenance fingerprint: a manifest built in a different tool environment is legitimately different.

profile_policy_digests.description: SHA-256 of each shipped profile-policy manifest, sorted by path.

schema_digests.description: SHA-256 of each shipped JSON schema, sorted by path.

canonicalization.description: How body is canonicalized before hashing, e.g. 'jq -S -c over .body'.

hash.description: SHA-256 over the canonical serialization of body.
